steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $5{x}^{2}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Both terms share the factor $x^2$. Subtract the coefficients: $14x^2 - 9x^2 = (14 - 9)x^2 = 5x^2$.

steps.title: Eliminate choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $-126{x}^{2}$: $-126$ would come from multiplying $14 \times (-9)$, not subtracting. ✗
  • Choice B — $5{x}^{2}$: $14 - 9 = 5$, so this is correct. ✓
  • Choice C — $23{x}^{2}$: $23$ would come from adding $14 + 9$, not subtracting. ✗
  • Choice D — $46{x}^{2}$: $46$ would come from doubling $14 + 9$, not subtracting. ✗
That matches Choice B — $5{x}^{2}$.
